sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What information contained in the engine Data Entry Plug?

options.text: N1 Modifier; EPR Modifier; Engine S/N; Engine rating.
EPR Modifier; Engine S/N; Engine rating; EEC P/N.
EPR Modifier; Engine S/N; Engine rating.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Which bleed valve is (are) fully modulating?

options.text: Stage 7 valves.
Stage 2.5.
Stage 10 valve.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Engine oil flow:

options.text: Always pass totally through the ACOC at all HMS modes.
Either totally or Not pass through ACOC depending on HMS modes.
Either partly or Not pass through ACOC depending on HMS modes.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What does the ACOC valve regulate?

options.text: The engine oil flowing through the ACOC.
The flow of fan air crossing the ACOC.
The flow of ram air crossing the ACOC.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What is the purpose of the No 4 bearing scavenge valve?

options.text: Scavenge No 4-bearing compartment.
Controls the venting/scavenge flow of air/oil mixture to the de-oiler.
Controls the ACAC (buffer) air flows for No 4 bearing compartment.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The HMS has how many modes?

options.text: One
Four
Five

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: In which HMS mode does the ACOC valve fully close?

options.text: Mode 1.
Mode 3.
Mode 5.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What is the failed safe position of the ACOC valve?

options.text: Fully opened.
Fully closed.
Stay at the last position.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The EEC controls the HMS with?

options.text: ACOC modulating valve and FD&RV and IDG FCOC.
ACOC modulating valve and FD&RV.
ACOC modulating valve; FD&RV and FCOC.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: In normal T/R operation, actuator unlocked condition is indicated by?

options.text: REV in green appears inside the EPR gauge.
REV in amber appears inside the N1 gauge.
REV in amber appears inside the EPR gauge.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The Overspeed valve and the PRSOV in the FMU are opened by?

options.text: Signals from the EEC to a modulating torque motor.
Signals from the EEC to a micro switches.
Metered fuel pressure.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How the Fuel distribution valve works?

options.text: It follows the EEC demand.
It is electrically controlled by EEC and fuel pressure operated
There’s no control of the valve.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The N1 speed signal is sensed from?

options.text: Speed probes interacting with a phonic wheel in the front bearing compartment.
A tachometer generator fitted into the accessory gearbox.
Speed probes located in the LP Turbine.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The indicated N2 speed is measured from?

options.text: Speed probes and a phonic wheel.
EEC computes value from frequency of 1 phase of the dedicated alternator (PMA) 3 phase output signal.
A tachometer generator fitted into the accessory gearbox.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What is the FADEC?

options.text: The interface between the Display Management Computer and the Engine Interface Unit.
The EEC, sensors and data input and the engine output devices such as fuel operated actuators and pneumatic valves.
The Electronic Engine Controller.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Is the same channel of the EEC, always in command?

options.text: Yes - except with channel failure.
No - command alternates between channels each hour.
No - command alternates between channels on consecutive flights.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: If a single data input signal to the EEC fails, will control revert to the other channel?

options.text: Yes - in all cases.
Yes - in certain identified conditions.
No - the input is used from the other channel.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: If both EEC channels failed, what engine power will automatically be set ?

options.text: TOGA, power or maximum power of the day.
Maximum climb power.
Approximately idle power.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How are the bearing compartments sealed?

options.text: Air supported labyrinth seals.
Air supported carbon seals.
Combination of labyrinth and hydraulic seals.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What additional task must be performed to supply power for the EEC when ground maintenance interrogation is required?

options.text: None
Select the FADEC ground power switch on, after energising the aircraft 28v DC bus – bar
Select emergency power by the rotary switch.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What provides the EEC channels A & B with a back – up N2 speed signal?

options.text: The frequency of the output of a dedicated single phase winding.
The Phonic wheel and Pulse probe arrangement on the LP stubshaft.
One of the three phase windings of Channel “B” power generation.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How is movement of the thrust lever sensed by the EEC?

options.text: Electrical signals from the dual output throttle resolver.
A cable and pulley input to resolvers in the EEC.
By dual thrust level rod input to the mechanical box.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What is the primary mode setting and controlling steady state engine power?

options.text: Fuel flow scheduled to deliver EPR - closed loop control.
Fuel flow scheduled to deliver N1 - closed loop control.
N1 set by thrust lever position – open loop.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How is the fuel flow signal transmitted from the EEC to the fuel metering valve?

options.text: Mechanically - thrust resolver angle (TRA).
Electrically - changes in electrical current value.
Electrically – changes in voltage level.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What power level is set if auto thrust is lost?

options.text: Forward idle.
Manual thrust lock position (Frozen).
TOGA power.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The HP fuel system is protected against excessive pressure by?

options.text: A relief valve venting excess pump delivery pressure to the pump inlet.
A relief valve in the FMU vents excess pressure to the diverter valve.
A bypass valve in the LP filter outlet case opens.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How is icing/waxing of the fuel prevented at low temperatures?

options.text: By a fuel heater in the HP fuel system.
Heat from the engine oil system transfers to the LP fuel in the fuel/oil heat exchanger.
By alteration of the fuel metering valve position and directly change the fuel flow to the combustion system.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How does the FMU schedule the fuel supply to the engine?

options.text: By positioning a pressure drop/spill valve to change the flow rate of return fuel to the LP system.
By changing the pump delivery rate to match the engine requirement.
By positioning of the fuel metering valve and directly change the fuel flow to the combustion system.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The EEC interface with the Fuel Metering Valve is by?

options.text: A two position torquemotor.
A three position solenoid.
A modulating torquemotor.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: where is the pressure drop/spill valve located?

options.text: In the HP fuel pump.
In the FMU.
In the fuel diverter valve.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: what positions the spill valve?

options.text: Signals from a modulatingtorquemotor.
Resolver signals from the fuel metering valve.
The pressure drop governor movement.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The Overspeed valve and the PRSOV are opened by?

options.text: Signals from the EEC to a modulating torquemotor.
Signals from the EEC to a micro switch.
Metered fuel pressure.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What letter should be visible in the “Switch window” of an FMU 8061 – 637 (V2530 & V2533)?

options.text: No letter displayed.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What range of engine thrust ratings is applicable to a “switchable” FMU, which is set to show ”O” in the switch window?

options.text: All possible engine ratings.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Is the fuel return to tank valve controlled by?

options.text: A two position torquemotor.
A solenoid.
A modulating torquemotor.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What flight conditions inhibit the fuel return to tank flow?

options.text: All conditions when engine is operating and aircraft is in flight.
High power conditions, take – off and early climb.
High altitude above 25,000 feet (7576m) in cruise,

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: In low temperature start conditions, how is the oil system protected from high pump delivery pressure?

options.text: A relief valve set at 450 psi, return pressure to the oil tank.
Excess pressure vents to the gearbox.
With the low oil temperature the pressure pump output is restricted.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How is the main cooling of the oil system achieved?

options.text: By an air - cooled oil cooler.
By a fuel - cooled oil cooler in the HP fuel system.
By as fuel - cooled oil cooler in the LP fuel system.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Is oil removed from the No 4 bearing chamber by?

options.text: A dedicated scavenge pump.
Air cooled air cooler (buffer) air.
Air pressure through the carbon seals.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Why is a compressor airflow control system used on the V2500?

options.text: To maintain adequate surge margin for compressor operation.
To give adequate bleed airflow for aircraft services.
To give stable operation of compressor at cruise condition.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: VIGV/VSV’s are fitted at which stages of the HP compressor?

options.text: Inlet, 3, 4, 5 & 6 (A1) and Inlet, 3, 4 & 5 (A5).

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The anti - ice control valve can be manually locked open for?

options.text: Despatch of aircraft with valve inoperative.
Engine operation when icing conditions will not be met.
Valve is never locked for any operating conditions.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The N1 (LP) speed signal is sensed from?

options.text: Speed probes interacting with a phonic wheel in the front bearing compartment.
A tachometer generator fitted into the accessory gearbox.
Speed probes located in the LP Turbine.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The indicated N2 speed is measured from?

options.text: Speed probes and a phonic wheel.
A dedicated frequency probe (primary) and the frequency of 1 phase of channel B, 3 phase power supply (secondary) of the dedicated alternator (PMA).
A tachometer generator fitted into the accessory gearbox.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What is the preferred bolt ring that the balancing weights should be attached to?

options.text: The 22 Annulus Filler bolt ring.
The 24 Nose Cone bolt ring.
The 36 Front Retaining Disc bolt ring.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How is access to the front blade retaining ring 24 bolt hole flange achieved?

options.text: Remove the 6 bolts retaining the Nose cone fairing and remove the fairing.
Remove the nose cone and fairing.
Remove the annulus filler.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How is the front blade retaining ring secured to the fan disk?

options.text: By 22 annulus filler safety bolts.
By a ring of 24 bolts.
By a ring of 36 bolts.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How is the front blade retaining ring removed from the fan disk?

options.text: By the use of 6 specially designed “extractor” bolts inserted into specially designed threaded holes.
By the use of 4 “puller” bolts inserted into the 36 bolt hole flange threaded holes.
By the use of a special “lever” tool.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Where on the fan blade is data recorded?

options.text: On the fan module data plate.
On the fan blade root.
On the blade front retaining plate.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: THE ENGINE OIL AND IDG OIL TEMPERATURES ARE CONTROLLED BY:

options.text: The engine fuel/oil and servo fuel heater.
Returning fuel to the center tank for cooling.
The Heat Management System, controlled by EEC.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: THE OIL VENT SYSTEM INCLUDES THE FOLLOWING COMPONENTS:

options.text: A de-aerator in the oil tank, and a de-oiler on the external gear box.
A de-oiler in the oil tank, and a de-aerator in the external gear box.
A de-oiler and de-aerator in the oil tank.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: THE FADEC PROVIDES TWO IDLE MODE SELECTION:

options.text: Flight idle and approach idle.
Approach idle and minimum idle.
Approach idle and landing idle.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: ON THE ECAM UPPER DISPLAY, THE WHITE CIRCLE MOVING AROUND THE ENGINE EPR GAUGE REPRESENTS:

options.text: The EPR limit.
The EPR actual.
The predicted EPR corresponding to the thrust lever position.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: THE RESOLVERS IN THE THROTTLE CONTROL UNIT PROVIDE THROTTLE RESOLVER ANGLE INFORMATION TO:

options.text: Channel A and channel B of the Engine Electronic Controller (EEC).
Channel A only of each EEC.
The Engine Interface Unit (EIU), then to the EEC.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: WITH THE ENGINE MASTER SWITCH "OFF":

options.text: The Pressure Raising and Shut-Off Valve (PRSOV) and the Low Pressure fuel shut-off valve are closed.
The High Pressure fuel valve is closed and Low Pressure fuel shut-off valve is open.
The PRSOV is open and the Low Pressure fuel shut-off valve is closed.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: IN CASE OF AN ENGINE INTERFACE UNIT FAILURE:

options.text: Engine start on the ground is not possible.
The FADEC reverts to the N1 control mode.
The engine runs at minimum fuel flow because of Engine Electronic Controller input loss.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: TO INITIATE A DRY CRANKING SEQUENCE, THE CORRECT SELECTION OF THE FOLLOWING COMPONENTS SHOULD BE:

options.text: Mode selector to IGN START and MAN START pushbutton selected ON.
Mode selector to CRANK and MAN START pushbutton selected ON.
Mode selector to CRANK, master switch ON, and MAN START pushbutton selected ON.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: THE ENGINE ELECTRONIC CONTROLLER CONTROLS SIX BLEED SOLENOID VALVES MOUNTED ON THE FAN CASE. ONE OF THESE SOLENOID VALVES PROVIDES:

The pneumatic system High Pressure Bleed Valve closure control.
The number 4 bearing scavenge valve pneumatic control.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: WHEN THE BOOSTER STAGE BLEED VALVE MASTER AND SLAVE ACTUATORS ARE REPLACED:

options.text: Only the master actuator may require adjustment.
No adjustment is required for the master actuator or slave actuator.
Both master and slave actuators may require adjustment.

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf

A FLEX temperature must be entered on the TAKE OFF PERF page of the MCDU

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: WITH FLYING IN N1 REVISIONARY MODE ON ENGINE #1, AUTOTHRUST IS AVAILABLE

options.text: True
False

REVERSER ISOLATION shown on ENGINE page
No indication in the cockpit

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf

options.text: Through CFDS via FADEC menus
By the lever installed on the valve
Can only be tested by starting the engine

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf

options.text: High EGT
Low oil pressure
No EGT rise

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf

options.text: Through a panel under the C-duct halves
Through the left fan cowl
Through a panel under the fan cowl

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf

options.text: Yes, it is possible by hand
No, it isn’t possible
Yes, it is possible by a special tool

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: ABOVE 50% N2, THE FUEL CUT OFF (ENGINE SHUT DOWN) COMMAND COMES FROM

options.text: The flight crew or engineer
Controlled by EEC
The flight crew or engineer and Controlled by EEC

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: IF AN OUTPUT FAUL IN ONE EEC CHANNEL IN CONTROL,…IF AN INPUT FAULT IN ONE EEC CHANNEL IN CONTROL,…

options.text: Cause switchover to control from the other channel That control channel uses input signal from the other channel
That control channel uses output signal from the other channel via crosstalk That control channel uses input signal from the other channel
Cause switchover to control from the other channel Cause switchover to control from the other channel

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: IF HANDLING BLEED VALVES (7thA, 7thB, 7thC, 10thSTAGE) STICK IN THE CLOSED POSITION, CAN IT BE DETECTED BY FADEC?

options.text: No, it is Not detected by FADEC
Yes, FADEC can detect directly by its sensor
Yes, FADEC can detect via Engine Interface Unit (EIU)

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf

options.text: Start valve, igniters and fuel flow
Start valve, igniters and HP fuel valve and fuel flow
Start valve, igniters, LP and HP fuel valve

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: IF ONE DAMAGED FAN BLADE HAS TO BE REPLACED

options.text: Replace by any fan blade out of MWD limit and do trim balance
Replace by the fan blade with (MWD) moment weight different in limit and do not trim balance
Both a and b

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: HOW TO REMOVE THE LP AND HP PUMP

options.text: LP and HP pump are removed individually
LP and HP pump are removed together
Remove the LP pump first then HP pump

The value of rate of change of burner pressure
Rapid decrease in burner pressure. The value of rate of change of burner pressure

7th bleed valves closed and 10th bleed vale opend
7th bleed valves opened and 10th bleed vale opened

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf

Set assumed temperature is lower than actual ambient temperature
Set assumed temperature is equal actual ambient temperature

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: HOW TO REPLACE N1 SENSOR

options.text: N1 is replaced on wing
N1 is replaced off wing
Both a and b

As function of N1

Electrically

sourceFile: 11. V2500 Engine CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf

No, it is totally electrical

PRSOV solenoid depowered close

by a hydraulic actuator using a manual hand pump.

options.text: In the pylon area.
